reviews.review_msg: Very tough grader, choses readings and subject material to his likings, readings very very difficult and confusing. Gives confusing feedback on what he wants. Gives tons of material every class. If you need a high gpa to enter into your program this is not the intructor for you, lots of homework, boring class time, wastes alot of class time.

Like many English profs he is a tough grader,  but he seems like he's on your side and wants you to improve. He's funny and relatable in class but you can tell he's smart and knows his stuff. Teaches you to write from a kind of formula, and you need to follow that formula in order to do well.

Very lecture and discussion heavy, but his powerpoints are solid. I don't know if it's normal for him to have few assignments as this course was not designed by him (he was subbing this course for a month and a half).  All in all, a solid prof. Would take again. Has a good sense of humor.

When he actually gives lectures he's interesting to listen to, but the majority of the time we just discuss pre-written questions with classmates and he doesn't teach anything. He's kind of a tough grader, and some of his criteria seems a bit arbitrary, which can be frustrating. Out of class he acts like he wants to help but doesn't really do much

Marc was a great prof; really tough marker, but he genuinely wanted to make sure he saw us improve, which was really encouraging. Still pending my final grade, but well worth taking the class, because I definitely became a better writer.

I think he failed to realize that the majortiy of the class was there to fulfill WQB requirements. His thinking was advanced, his expecations extremely high, and his lectures unclear and often taken off on weird tangents. Keep up with ALL the required readings and make sure you understand what he is looking for on assignments.

Dr. Acherman's lectures were honestly my favourite this semester. And that's coming from a science major! His lectures were thought provoking and mind boggling. Yeah, the powerpoints were threadbare, but that motivates you to go to class and listen to what he has to say about the readings, which were always so interesting. Definitely recommended!

Marc was an amazing prof, engaging and always positive. He made effort to make any dry content more interesting with videos. He cared a lot about the well being of the class. Be prepared to read a lot, but that's expected for an English class/W credit. Definitely take a class with Marc if you can!

English 105 W was a tougher course than I had anticipated. He tried to make the lectures interesting, but he isn't as funny as he thinks. His lecture slides do not have enough detail, and he speaks too fast to notes and says that we can always go back to the slides, the slides are very limited on the information that they give.
